tasks.subject: [marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P1] Add Neon serverless and Drizzle ORM dependencies to apps/site
tasks.description:
Add `@neondatabase/serverless` and `drizzle-orm` as production dependencies of `apps/site`, plus `drizzle-kit` as a devDependency for migrations. These power the marketplace install telemetry pipeline (single source of truth, no Redis).

IMPORTANT: `@vercel/kv`, `@vercel/postgres`, and `@upstash/redis` are NOT used. We use `@neondatabase/serverless` (HTTP driver, edge-compatible) with `drizzle-orm/neon-http` for type-safe queries. This is consistent with `packages/db` which already uses Drizzle for SQLite.

## Files to modify

- `apps/site/package.json` — add dependencies
- `pnpm-lock.yaml` — refresh lockfile

## Implementation Steps

1. From repo root run: `pnpm add @neondatabase/serverless drizzle-orm --filter @dorkos/site`
2. Add drizzle-kit as devDep: `pnpm add -D drizzle-kit --filter @dorkos/site`
3. Verify `apps/site/package.json` has `@neondatabase/serverless` and `drizzle-orm` in `dependencies`, and `drizzle-kit` in `devDependencies`
4. Run `pnpm install` to refresh the lockfile
5. Run `pnpm typecheck --filter @dorkos/site` to verify no breakage
6. Verify `grep -rn '@vercel/kv|@vercel/postgres|@upstash/redis' apps/site/ packages/marketplace/` returns nothing

## Environment Variables (provisioned in task 5.2)

- `DATABASE_URL` — Neon Postgres connection string

No Upstash, no `TELEMETRY_QUEUE_URL`. The Edge Function writes directly to Postgres via Drizzle (single hop).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `apps/site/package.json` lists `@neondatabase/serverless` and `drizzle-orm` in `dependencies`
- [ ] `apps/site/package.json` lists `drizzle-kit` in `devDependencies`
- [ ] `pnpm-lock.yaml` updated
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck --filter @dorkos/site` passes
- [ ] No references to `@vercel/kv`, `@vercel/postgres`, or `@upstash/redis`
tasks.activeForm: Adding Neon serverless and Drizzle ORM dependencies

tasks.subject: [marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P1] Scaffold marketplace feature module under apps/site
tasks.description:
Create the FSD layer scaffold for the marketplace feature inside `apps/site`. This mirrors the existing `apps/site/src/layers/features/marketing/` module pattern.

## Files to create

```
apps/site/src/layers/features/marketplace/
├── lib/
│   ├── fetch.ts            # placeholder, implemented in 2.1
│   ├── ranking.ts          # placeholder, implemented in 2.2
│   ├── telemetry.ts        # placeholder, implemented in 5.2
│   └── format-permissions.ts  # placeholder, implemented in 3.3
├── ui/
│   └── .gitkeep            # populated in Phase 3
└── index.ts                # barrel
```

## Barrel `index.ts` content

```typescript
/**
 * Marketplace feature module — apps/site marketplace browse and detail experience.
 *
 * Provides server-side fetch helpers, ranking, telemetry queries, and UI components
 * for the public `/marketplace` browse page and `/marketplace/[slug]` detail pages.
 *
 * @module features/marketplace
 */

export { fetchMarketplaceJson, fetchPackageReadme } from './lib/fetch';
export { rankPackages } from './lib/ranking';
export { fetchInstallCount, fetchInstallCounts } from './lib/telemetry';
export { formatPermissions } from './lib/format-permissions';
```

## Placeholder file contents

Each `lib/*.ts` file should be created as an empty stub with a TSDoc module comment and a `// TODO(spec 04 task X.Y)` marker so the imports compile against `index.ts`. Example for `fetch.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Marketplace registry fetch helpers — pulls marketplace.json from
 * dorkos-community and per-package READMEs from raw GitHub URLs.
 *
 * @module features/marketplace/lib/fetch
 */

import type { MarketplaceJson } from '@dorkos/marketplace';

// TODO(spec 04 task 2.1): implement fetchMarketplaceJson
export async function fetchMarketplaceJson(): Promise<MarketplaceJson> {
  throw new Error('Not implemented');
}

// TODO(spec 04 task 2.1): implement fetchPackageReadme
export async function fetchPackageReadme(_sourceUrl: string): Promise<string> {
  throw new Error('Not implemented');
}
```

Follow the same pattern for `ranking.ts`, `telemetry.ts`, and `format-permissions.ts`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `apps/site/src/layers/features/marketplace/` directory exists with the structure shown
- [ ] `index.ts` barrel exports compile against the placeholder stubs
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck --filter @dorkos/site` passes
- [ ] No new lint warnings introduced
- [ ] All four placeholder lib files have proper TSDoc module headers

tasks.subject: [marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P1] Document the dorkos-community registry repo layout in contributing/marketplace-registry.md
tasks.description:
Create a new contributing guide that documents the `dorkos-community/marketplace` registry repo structure. This is the contract document the eventual GitHub repo creation will follow, and it lives in-repo so it survives without depending on external GitHub state.

## File to create

`contributing/marketplace-registry.md`

## Required sections

1. **Overview** — One paragraph explaining the registry's purpose: source of truth for the public marketplace catalog, hosted at `github.com/dorkos-community/marketplace`, consumed by `dorkos.ai/marketplace` via hourly ISR.
2. **Repo layout** — Reproduce the file tree from spec section "Registry Repo (`dorkos-community/marketplace`)":

```
dorkos-community/marketplace/
├── marketplace.json                  # The registry index
├── README.md                         # Public-facing description
├── CONTRIBUTING.md                   # How to submit a package
├── CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
├── LICENSE                           # MIT
└── .github/
    └── workflows/
        ├── validate-submission.yml   # Runs `dorkos package validate` on PRs
        └── publish-update.yml        # Notifies dorkos.ai when registry changes
```

3. **`marketplace.json` schema** — Link to `@dorkos/marketplace/marketplace-json-schema.ts` and reproduce the 8-package seed payload from the spec verbatim under a `<details>` block.
4. **Submission flow** — Reproduce the CONTRIBUTING.md submission checklist from the spec verbatim.
5. **GitHub Actions** — Reproduce the `validate-submission.yml` workflow YAML from the spec.
6. **Branch protection** — Document required settings: `main` requires PR review, status checks, and `validate-submission.yml` to pass.
7. **CODEOWNERS** — `* @dorkos-community/maintainers`

## Cross-references

- Link to `specs/marketplace-04-web-and-registry/02-specification.md`
- Link to `packages/marketplace/src/marketplace-json-schema.ts`
- Link to `packages/cli/src/commands/` for the validate-marketplace and validate-remote commands (added in 7.1)

## Update AGENTS.md

Add this guide to the AGENTS.md guides table under the marketplace row:

```markdown
| [`contributing/marketplace-registry.md`](contributing/marketplace-registry.md) | dorkos-community registry repo layout, marketplace.json schema, submission flow |
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `contributing/marketplace-registry.md` exists with all 7 sections
- [ ] All code blocks (JSON, YAML, file tree) are copied verbatim from the spec
- [ ] AGENTS.md guides table includes the new entry
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes (markdown lint may apply)

tasks.subject: [marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P1] Define Drizzle schema and migration for marketplace_install_events
tasks.description: See task #29 in the live task system for the full implementation. Schema lives at apps/site/src/db/schema.ts (NOT packages/db). Creates marketplaceInstallEvents pgTable with id, packageName, marketplace, type, outcome, durationMs, errorCode, installId, dorkosVersion, receivedAt, plus indexes on (package_name, received_at DESC) and (marketplace, received_at DESC). Adds apps/site/src/db/client.ts (lazy getDb), apps/site/drizzle.config.ts, and runs drizzle-kit generate to produce the initial migration. Schema test enforces privacy-by-schema with negative assertions on ipAddress, userAgent, hostname, username, cwd. Adds db:generate, db:migrate, db:studio scripts to apps/site/package.json. Depends on 1.1.

tasks.subject: [marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P2] Implement fetchMarketplaceJson and fetchPackageReadme
[marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P2] Implement rankPackages function
[marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P2] Implement telemetry read helpers (fetchInstallCount, fetchInstallCounts)
tasks.description:
Implement the telemetry read helpers in `apps/site/src/layers/features/marketplace/lib/telemetry.ts`. These query Neon Postgres via Drizzle (the same DB the Edge Function writes to) and provide the install counts that `rankPackages` consumes.

This is **single source of truth** — no Redis, no separate counter store. The query runs at hourly ISR refresh time only.

## File to modify

`apps/site/src/layers/features/marketplace/lib/telemetry.ts`

## Implementation

Uses Drizzle's `db.select({ count: sql<number>\`count(*)::int\` }).from(marketplaceInstallEvents).where(and(eq(marketplace, 'dorkos-community'), eq(outcome, 'success'))).groupBy(packageName)`.

Two exports:
- `fetchInstallCount(packageName)` — returns count for one package (filtered by marketplace='dorkos-community' AND outcome='success')
- `fetchInstallCounts()` — returns Record<string, number> for all packages

Both use `getDb()` from `@/db/client` lazily so unit tests work without DATABASE_URL set.

See task #6 in the live task system for the full code.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Both functions implemented with Drizzle (NOT Redis)
- [ ] Both filter by outcome='success' so failed installs don't inflate counts
- [ ] Both scoped to marketplace='dorkos-community'
- [ ] Tests mock @/db/client; cover empty result, one row, group by
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck --filter @dorkos/site` passes

[marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P2] Add /marketplace/privacy page
tasks.description:
Create a static page documenting the marketplace telemetry privacy contract. This page is linked from `MarketplaceHeader` and from the in-product telemetry consent toggle (Phase 6). ## File to create `apps/site/src/app/(marketing)/marketplace/privacy/page.tsx` ## Content Reproduce the spec's "Privacy guarantees" list verbatim, framed as a public document. ```tsx import type { Metadata } from 'next'; import Link from 'next/link'; import { siteConfig } from '@/config/site'; export const metadata: Metadata = { title: 'Marketplace privacy — DorkOS', description: 'How DorkOS handles install telemetry. Opt-in. No PII. Anonymous install IDs. Open source pipeline.', alternates: { canonical: '/marketplace/privacy' }, openGraph: { title: 'Marketplace privacy — DorkOS', description: 'How DorkOS handles install telemetry — opt-in, no PII, anonymous IDs.', url: '/marketplace/privacy', siteName: siteConfig.name, }, }; export default function MarketplacePrivacyPage() { return (

Marketplace privacy

What DorkOS does — and does not — collect when you install a marketplace package.

The contract

  • No IP addresses logged.
  • No user identifiers — only random per-install UUIDs (one per install, not per user).
  • No package contents transmitted.
  • Opt-in. Disabled by default. You enable it explicitly in DorkOS settings.
  • Aggregate counts only displayed publicly on marketplace pages.
  • Full event data accessible only to the DorkOS team for debugging.

What we collect when you opt in

  • The package name and marketplace it came from.
  • The package type (agent, plugin, skill-pack, adapter).
  • The install outcome (success, failure, cancelled).
  • How long the install took (for performance debugging).
  • An error code on failure (so we can fix what is breaking).
  • A random install ID generated locally per install.
  • The DorkOS version (so we can spot version-specific install regressions).

How to opt in (or out)

The toggle lives in DorkOS settings. The default is off. The DorkOS client never reports telemetry until you flip the switch.

Open source pipeline

Both the client-side reporter and the receiving Edge Function live in the public DorkOS repo. You can audit exactly what gets sent.

Back to the marketplace

); } ``` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Page renders at `/marketplace/privacy` - [ ] Lists all 6 privacy guarantees from the spec verbatim - [ ] Documents the data collected on opt-in - [ ] Mentions the open source pipeline - [ ] `metadata` set with canonical + openGraph - [ ] `pnpm typecheck --filter @dorkos/site` passes

tasks.subject: [marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P2] Add OG image generators for /marketplace and /marketplace/[slug]

[marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P2] Implement /api/telemetry/install Edge Function
tasks.description:
Implement the install telemetry endpoint as a Vercel Edge Function. Validates with Zod, then writes a single row into `marketplace_install_events` via Drizzle. **No Redis. No queue forwarding.**

## File to create

`apps/site/src/app/api/telemetry/install/route.ts`

## Implementation

`runtime = 'edge'`. POST handler:
1. Parse JSON body, return 400 on parse failure
2. Validate against InstallEventSchema (Zod), return 400 with issues on schema failure
3. `db.insert(marketplaceInstallEvents).values({ ...event })` via Drizzle
4. Wrap insert in try/catch — log errors and return 200 anyway (graceful degradation, no client retry storm)
5. Return `{ ok: true }` with 200

All three outcomes (success, failure, cancelled) are inserted — failures are debugging signal.

See task #15 in the live task system for the full code.

## Privacy assertion test

Constructs a request with PII-shaped headers (x-forwarded-for, cookie, user-agent) and verifies that none of those values appear in the values passed to db.insert(...).values(...).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Endpoint at apps/site/src/app/api/telemetry/install/route.ts
- [ ] runtime = 'edge'
- [ ] Uses Drizzle insert (NOT Redis, NOT queue forward)
- [ ] All 3 outcomes inserted
- [ ] DB errors caught, request returns 200
- [ ] Privacy assertion test passes
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck --filter @dorkos/site` passes

tasks.subject: [marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P2] Document Vercel integrations and env var setup in contributing guide
tasks.description:
Document the Vercel Marketplace integration needed for telemetry (Neon Postgres only — no Upstash) and the Drizzle migration workflow.

## File to create

`contributing/marketplace-telemetry.md`

## Required sections

1. **Overview** — opt-in pipeline, Edge Function writes directly to Neon via Drizzle, no Redis, no queues
2. **Required Vercel integration** — `vercel integration add neon` (provisions DATABASE_URL). Note that @vercel/kv, @vercel/postgres, @upstash/redis are NOT used.
3. **Environment variables** — table with DATABASE_URL only
4. **Local development** — DATABASE_URL not required locally; page degrades to zero counts via .catch
5. **Drizzle schema and migrations** — pnpm db:generate, pnpm db:migrate, pnpm db:studio. Initial migration committed at apps/site/drizzle/0000_*.sql.
6. **Schema reference** — reproduce the marketplaceInstallEvents pgTable definition verbatim
7. **Privacy contract** — schema is the contract; allowed columns enforced by schema test with negative PII assertions; cross-reference end-to-end privacy test
8. **Aggregation strategy** — single GROUP BY at hourly ISR refresh; future-state mitigation is atomic counter table via INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE; aggregate to daily after 30 days

## Update AGENTS.md

Add to guides table: contributing/marketplace-telemetry.md with summary "Marketplace install telemetry: Neon + Drizzle setup, schema, privacy contract".

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 8 sections present
- [ ] AGENTS.md guides table updated
- [ ] Commands and schema reproduced verbatim
- [ ] Zero references to @vercel/kv, @vercel/postgres, @upstash/redis, or Vercel Queues
- [ ] Privacy section references both the schema test and end-to-end test
tasks.activeForm: Documenting Vercel integrations and env vars

tasks.subject: [marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P2] Add telemetry config setting to DorkOS server config
tasks.description:
Add a `telemetry.enabled` boolean to the DorkOS server config, defaulting to `false`. This is the user-facing opt-in switch that controls whether the telemetry reporter forwards install events to dorkos.ai.

## Files to modify

- `packages/shared/src/config-schema.ts` (or whichever file owns the runtime config Zod schema)
- `apps/server/src/services/config/` — wherever config is loaded and merged

## Schema change

Add a `telemetry` block to the existing config schema:

```typescript
telemetry: z
  .object({
    /**
     * Whether to send opt-in marketplace install events to dorkos.ai.
     *
     * Defaults to `false`. The user must explicitly enable this in their
     * config file or via the in-product toggle. Privacy contract:
     * https://dorkos.ai/marketplace/privacy
     */
    enabled: z.boolean().default(false),
  })
  .default({ enabled: false }),
```

## Discovery

First run `grep -rn 'config-schema' packages/shared/src/` and `grep -rn 'ConfigSchema' apps/server/src/` to find the existing schema location. Slot the new field into the existing structure rather than inventing a parallel config file.

## Migration

No migration needed — the field defaults to `false` for existing configs that do not declare it.

## Tests

- Schema test: a config without `telemetry` parses with `telemetry.enabled === false`
- Schema test: a config with `telemetry.enabled: true` parses correctly
- Schema test: a config with `telemetry: { enabled: 'yes' }` (string) fails validation

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `telemetry.enabled` field added with default `false`
- [ ] All existing config tests still pass
- [ ] New schema tests added and passing
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes across the workspace
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes

tasks.subject: [marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P2] Implement registerDorkosCommunityTelemetry server-side reporter
[marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P2] Surface telemetry consent toggle in Dork Hub UI
tasks.description:
Surface a clear, non-deceptive telemetry consent toggle in the Dork Hub UI built in spec 03. The toggle controls `config.telemetry.enabled` via the existing config API. ## Files to modify / create - `apps/client/src/widgets/marketplace/` — find the existing Dork Hub layout (likely `DorkHubPage.tsx` or similar) - `apps/client/src/layers/features/marketplace/ui/TelemetryConsentBanner.tsx` (new) - `apps/client/src/layers/entities/config/model/` — likely already has `useConfig`/`useUpdateConfig` hooks; verify before creating ## Discovery first Before creating new code, verify: 1. `find apps/client/src -name 'DorkHub*'` to locate the Dork Hub page 2. `grep -rn 'useConfig\|useUpdateConfig' apps/client/src/layers/entities/config/` to find the existing config hooks 3. If no config entity hooks exist, this task should also create them (small additional scope) ## TelemetryConsentBanner.tsx A dismissible banner shown above the Dork Hub package grid until the user has either opted in or explicitly opted out. ```tsx import { useConfig, useUpdateConfig } from '@/layers/entities/config'; import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button'; /** * Telemetry consent banner — shown above the Dork Hub package grid until the * user makes a decision. Default state is opt-out; the user must take an action * to opt in. */ export function TelemetryConsentBanner() { const { data: config } = useConfig(); const updateConfig = useUpdateConfig(); // Hide once the user has explicitly set the value (true or false after dismiss) if (config?.telemetry?.userHasDecided) return null; return (

Help improve the marketplace

Send anonymous install events to dorkos.ai so we can rank packages and fix install failures. No PII. Random per-install ID. Off unless you opt in.{' '} Privacy contract .

); } ``` ## Schema addition The `userHasDecided` field requires extending the `telemetry` config block from task 6.1: ```typescript telemetry: z.object({ enabled: z.boolean().default(false), /** True once the user has explicitly chosen (so the banner stops showing). */ userHasDecided: z.boolean().default(false), }).default({ enabled: false, userHasDecided: false }), ``` This is a minor extension to 6.1 — apply it as part of this task if 6.1 has already merged, or coordinate during execution. ## Wire into Dork Hub page At the top of the Dork Hub page body, add: ```tsx ``` ## Persistent settings link Even after the banner is dismissed, users need a way to flip telemetry on/off. Add a small link in the Dork Hub header or sidebar (find the existing settings entry point in the app shell): ```tsx Telemetry settings ``` If there is no `/settings` route yet, file a follow-up issue rather than building it here. Core acceptance is the banner. ## Tests `apps/client/src/layers/features/marketplace/ui/__tests__/TelemetryConsentBanner.test.tsx` - Banner renders when `userHasDecided === false` - Banner does not render when `userHasDecided === true` - Clicking "No thanks" calls `updateConfig` with `enabled: false, userHasDecided: true` - Clicking "Send anonymous events" calls `updateConfig` with `enabled: true, userHasDecided: true` - Privacy link opens in new tab with `rel="noopener noreferrer"` Use the standard React Testing Library + mock TanStack Query setup. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] `TelemetryConsentBanner` component exists with TSDoc - [ ] Schema extended with `userHasDecided` field - [ ] Banner appears once on first marketplace visit - [ ] Banner dismisses persistently (via config write) - [ ] Privacy link points to `dorkos.ai/marketplace/privacy` - [ ] All component tests pass - [ ] `pnpm test --filter @dorkos/client` passes - [ ] `pnpm typecheck --filter @dorkos/client` passes

tasks.subject: [marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P2] Author marketplace.json seed file and submission workflow YAML
tasks.description:
Author the canonical `marketplace.json` seed payload and the GitHub Actions `validate-submission.yml` workflow as in-repo fixtures so the dorkos-community repo can be bootstrapped from these files. The actual `dorkos-community/marketplace` repo creation is a Phase 8 deploy step — this task produces the source files that will live in that repo.

## Files to create

- `packages/marketplace/fixtures/dorkos-community-marketplace.json`
- `packages/marketplace/fixtures/validate-submission.yml`
- `packages/marketplace/fixtures/CONTRIBUTING.md`
- `packages/marketplace/fixtures/README.md`

## dorkos-community-marketplace.json

Reproduce the spec's seed payload verbatim:

```json
{
  "name": "dorkos-community",
  "description": "Official community marketplace for DorkOS — agents, plugins, skill packs, and adapters",
  "plugins": [
    {
      "name": "code-reviewer",
      "source": "https://github.com/dorkos-community/code-reviewer",
      "description": "Reviews your PRs every weekday morning, posts findings to Slack, files Linear issues for blockers",
      "type": "agent",
      "category": "code-quality",
      "tags": ["review", "pr", "ci"],
      "icon": "🔍",
      "featured": true
    },
    {
      "name": "security-auditor",
      "source": "https://github.com/dorkos-community/security-auditor",
      "description": "Weekly dependency vulnerability scans, secret detection, and license compliance audits",
      "type": "agent",
      "category": "security",
      "tags": ["audit", "security", "dependencies"],
      "icon": "🛡️",
      "featured": true
    },
    {
      "name": "docs-keeper",
      "source": "https://github.com/dorkos-community/docs-keeper",
      "description": "Watches code changes, suggests documentation updates, keeps READMEs in sync with reality",
      "type": "agent",
      "category": "documentation",
      "tags": ["docs", "maintenance"],
      "icon": "📚",
      "featured": true
    },
    {
      "name": "linear-integration",
      "source": "https://github.com/dorkos-community/linear-integration",
      "description": "Linear status dashboard extension and webhook adapter for issue notifications",
      "type": "plugin",

      "tags": ["linear", "issues"],
      "layers": ["extensions", "adapters"],
      "icon": "📋"
    },
    {
      "name": "posthog-monitor",
      "source": "https://github.com/dorkos-community/posthog-monitor",
      "description": "PostHog dashboard widget and error alerting for your DorkOS sidebar",
      "type": "plugin",
      "category": "observability",
      "tags": ["analytics", "monitoring", "errors"],
      "layers": ["extensions", "tasks"],
      "icon": "📊"
    },
    {
      "name": "security-audit-pack",
      "source": "https://github.com/dorkos-community/security-audit-pack",
      "description": "Scheduled security audit tasks: dependency scanning, secret detection, license checks",
      "type": "skill-pack",
      "category": "security",
      "tags": ["audit", "tasks"],
      "layers": ["tasks"],
      "icon": "🔐"
    },
    {
      "name": "release-pack",
      "source": "https://github.com/dorkos-community/release-pack",
      "description": "Tasks for version bumping, changelog generation, and git tagging",

      "layers": ["tasks", "skills"],
      "icon": "🚀"
    },

      "description": "Discord relay adapter — bridge agent messages to Discord channels and DMs",

      "category": "messaging",
      "tags": ["discord", "chat"],
      "layers": ["adapters"],
      "icon": "💬"
    }
  ]
}
```

## validate-submission.yml

```yaml
name: Validate Submission
on:
  pull_request:
    paths:
      - 'marketplace.json'

jobs:
  validate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: pnpm/action-setup@v4
      - name: Install dorkos CLI
        run: pnpm install -g dorkos
      - name: Validate marketplace.json
        run: dorkos package validate-marketplace marketplace.json
      - name: Validate each new package
        run: |
          for pkg in $(jq -r '.plugins[].source' marketplace.json); do
            dorkos package validate-remote "$pkg"
          done
```

## CONTRIBUTING.md

Reproduce the spec's CONTRIBUTING.md content verbatim (the full submission flow text from the spec).

## README.md

A short introduction explaining the registry repo's purpose and linking to dorkos.ai/marketplace and the CONTRIBUTING.md.

## Fixture validation test

Add `packages/marketplace/src/__tests__/seed-fixture.test.ts`:

- Loads `fixtures/dorkos-community-marketplace.json` from disk
- Asserts `parseMarketplaceJson(content).ok === true`
- Asserts the parsed result has exactly 8 plugins
- Asserts every plugin has the required `name`, `source`, `description`, `type`
- Asserts the type distribution is exactly 3 agents, 2 plugins, 2 skill-packs, 1 adapter

This test enforces that the seed payload stays valid as the schema evolves.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 4 fixture files created
- [ ] Seed JSON contains exactly 8 packages with correct type distribution
- [ ] Fixture validation test passes
- [ ] `pnpm test --filter @dorkos/marketplace` passes

tasks.subject: [marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P3] Lighthouse + accessibility audit for /marketplace
tasks.description:
Run a Lighthouse audit against `/marketplace` and `/marketplace/code-reviewer` and capture the results to verify the spec's acceptance criteria: LCP < 2.5s and accessibility score 100.

## Approach

Use the `chrome-devtools-mcp:debug-optimize-lcp` and `chrome-devtools-mcp:a11y-debugging` skills to run the audit against a local `pnpm dev` instance of `apps/site`. This is a manual one-shot verification, not a recurring CI test.

## Steps

1. Start `pnpm dev --filter @dorkos/site`
2. Use chrome-devtools-mcp's `lighthouse_audit` against `http://localhost:3000/marketplace`
3. Capture the JSON report; assert LCP metric < 2500ms and accessibility category score === 1.0
4. Repeat for `/marketplace/code-reviewer`
5. If LCP > 2.5s or accessibility < 100, file follow-up issues in Linear with the failing audit attachments

## Document the results

Create `research/20260407_marketplace_lighthouse_baseline.md` with:
- Date of audit
- Both audit JSON outputs (or links)
- Pass/fail per acceptance criterion
- Any follow-up issues filed

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Lighthouse audit run against both pages
- [ ] Results captured in `research/20260407_marketplace_lighthouse_baseline.md`
- [ ] LCP < 2500ms verified for both pages, OR follow-up issue filed with reproduction
- [ ] Accessibility score === 100 verified for both pages, OR follow-up issue filed with reproduction
- [ ] Research file linked from the spec's Acceptance Criteria checklist

### Marketplace web and registry

tasks.subject: [marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P4] Update AGENTS.md routes table and run /adr:from-spec
tasks.description:
Update AGENTS.md to document the new marketplace routes on apps/site and the new Neon+Drizzle integration, then extract architectural decisions into ADRs.

## AGENTS.md updates

1. Add 4 new routes to the apps/site routing description:
   - /marketplace → public browse page (server-rendered, hourly ISR)
   - /marketplace/[slug] → per-package detail with README + OG image
   - /marketplace/privacy → install telemetry privacy contract
   - /api/telemetry/install → Edge Function writing to Neon via Drizzle

2. Add a new line under apps/site noting the database integration:
   "apps/site database: Neon Postgres + Drizzle ORM. Schema at apps/site/src/db/schema.ts. Migrations under apps/site/drizzle/. Run pnpm db:generate after schema changes."

3. Update Marketplace service domain to mention web/registry layer.

4. Verify both contributing guides are in the guides table.

## Run /adr:from-spec

Extract ADRs. REQUIRED:
- **ADR: Use Neon + Drizzle as single source of truth (no Redis)** — records the 2026-04-07 Changelog decision. Why: ORM consistency with packages/db, future-proof, hourly ISR makes counters unnecessary, simpler infra. Explicitly mentions @vercel/kv, @vercel/postgres, @upstash/redis are avoided.
- **ADR: Site-local Drizzle schema (apps/site/src/db) instead of packages/db** — Why: packages/db is SQLite-only.

Optional:
- ADR: PR-based registry submissions in v1
- ADR: Opt-in telemetry default-off
- ADR: Featured + log(install_count) ranking

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] AGENTS.md routes table updated
- [ ] apps/site database section added
- [ ] Both contributing guides in guides table
- [ ] At least 2 ADRs created (Neon+Drizzle and site-local schema)
- [ ] decisions/manifest.json updated
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes

tasks.subject: [marketplace-04-web-and-registry] [P4] Create dorkos-community GitHub org and bootstrap registry repo
tasks.description:
External deploy task: provision the public `dorkos-community` GitHub organization and seed the `marketplace` repository with the fixtures from task 7.2. This is a deployment step rather than a code change — the fixtures already live in-repo, this task moves them into their final home.

## Pre-requisites

- Task 7.2 complete (fixture files in `packages/marketplace/fixtures/`)
- Task 7.1 complete (CLI commands the workflow depends on)
- A GitHub account with org-creation permission

## Steps

1. Create the GitHub organization `dorkos-community` (manual UI step)
2. Create the `marketplace` repository under that org, public, MIT license
3. Push the fixture files to the new repo:

```bash
git init dorkos-community-marketplace
cd dorkos-community-marketplace
cp ../packages/marketplace/fixtures/dorkos-community-marketplace.json marketplace.json
cp ../packages/marketplace/fixtures/CONTRIBUTING.md .
cp ../packages/marketplace/fixtures/README.md .
mkdir -p .github/workflows
cp ../packages/marketplace/fixtures/validate-submission.yml .github/workflows/
# Copy LICENSE (MIT) and CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md from a standard template
git add .
git commit -m "chore: bootstrap dorkos-community marketplace registry"
git branch -M main
git remote add origin git@github.com:dorkos-community/marketplace.git
git push -u origin main
```

4. Configure branch protection on `main`:
   - Require pull request before merge
   - Require status checks: `validate-submission`
   - Require linear history
   - Dismiss stale PR approvals on new commits
5. Add CODEOWNERS file: `* @dorkos-community/maintainers`
6. Create the `maintainers` team in the org and add at least one member

## Verify the live fetch

After the repo is live, run from the dorkos repo:

```bash
curl -sf https://raw.githubusercontent.com/dorkos-community/marketplace/main/marketplace.json | jq '.plugins | length'
# Expected: 8
```

Then visit `https://dorkos.ai/marketplace` (after the next deploy) and verify all 8 packages render.

## Note on seed package repos

The 8 individual seed package repos (`code-reviewer`, `security-auditor`, etc.) are out of scope for this spec — they are tracked as a separate engineering effort per the spec's Phase 2 note: "A separate engineering effort builds these." This task ONLY creates the registry repo. The actual install paths to the packages will 404 until the seed package repos exist; that is expected.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `github.com/dorkos-community/marketplace` exists and is publicly browsable
- [ ] `marketplace.json` is fetchable from the raw URL and contains 8 packages
- [ ] Branch protection configured on `main`
- [ ] CODEOWNERS file present
- [ ] `maintainers` team exists with at least one member
- [ ] Live `dorkos.ai/marketplace` page renders all 8 packages after the next deploy
